principle: Group related content into visually distinct chunks using proximity, dividers, whitespace and hierarchy — users scan chunks not individual items
explanation: X.ux|chunking: working_memory == limited_buffer; chunk == group_of_related_items_perceived_as_one_unit; apply: proximity + dividers + whitespace + color + scale -> distinct_visual_groups; user_scans_chunks -> finds_relevant_group -> reads_details; NO:wall_of_text NO:flat_list_without_grouping eg: "20_filters_in_flat_list" >> "filters_grouped_by_category_with_dividers_like_nike_sidebar"

principle: Reduce cognitive load by making interface patterns learnable once — every novel interaction pattern consumes working memory slots needed for the actual task
explanation: X.ux|cognitive_load: working_memory == finite_slots; learning_interface_pattern -> consumes_slots -> fewer_slots_for_user_task; minimize: use_familiar_patterns + consistent_layout + clear_affordances; overload -> missed_details + frustration + task_abandonment; NO:novel_interaction_per_screen NO:inconsistent_layouts eg: "each_page_different_nav_position" >> "consistent_nav+layout -> user_learns_once -> all_memory_for_task"

principle: Do not limit visible navigation to seven citing Miller — visible menus need no memorization but still group and prioritize to reduce choice time
explanation: X.ux|seven_myth: millers_7+-2 == memory_limit NOT perception_limit; visible_menu: items_on_screen -> zero_memorization -> no_miller_cap; BUT hicks_law: more_options -> slower_decision; balance: show_needed_links + chunk_by_category + clear_hierarchy -> scannable_without_arbitrary_cap; NO:hiding_links_to_stay_under_7 NO:flat_20_item_nav_without_grouping eg: "8_links_hidden_in_hamburger_citing_miller" >> "12_links_visible_chunked_into_3_groups"
